parameters.content:
Create Generic Credential
Type: Query Auth
Name: token
Your Browserless API Key

allowedDomains
Only crawl URLs from specified root domains.
https://ready.gov/
→ ready.gov, fema.gov,

allowedSubdomains
Only crawl specific subdomains (instead of all).
https://nasa.gov/
→ science.nasa.gov, nasa.gov,

blockedSubdomains
Crawl all subdomains except those listed.
https://nasa.gov/
→ plus.nasa.gov

allowedSections
Only crawl URLs containing specified path segments.
https://nps.gov/subjects/fossils/
→ fossils, articles

blockedSections
Crawl all sections except those containing specified path segments.
https://ready.gov/
→ ar,es,fr,ht,ja,ko,ru,tl,vi,zh-hans,hi,pt-br,

dataTypes
Regex filter for allowed file types.
e.g., allow: HTML, PDF (customizable)

Batch
Batch size for HTTP Request Nodes

## DataPreview
Simulates a crawl + scrape to show what URLs and content would be collected before a full run.
There are multiple filters available in the Sets Inputs node to help refine what data is collected during a run.

## HTTP Path

Uses a standard HTTP request to fetch the page.

Shows:
• Extracted URLs
• Full Markdown output of the page
• Word count (for content comparison)

Represents what a typical crawler sees without JavaScript rendering.

## Browserless Path (Optional)

Renders the page in a headless browser environment.

Can detect:
• JavaScript-rendered content
• Hidden interactive text
• Dynamically injected links

Shows:
• Extracted URLs
• Full Markdown output
• Word count (compare against HTTP path)

Useful for identifying content missed by standard requests.

## Duplicate Detection

Identifies duplicate URLs found during extraction.

Helps:
• Prevent redundant crawling
• Reduce unnecessary scraping
• Keep final dataset clean

Duplicates are flagged before final merge.

## Filters

Applies the same rules as the Crawl workflow:

• Domain filters
• Subdomain filters
• Section (path) filters
• Data type filters

Ensures preview results match real crawl behavior.

## URL Validation (HEAD Check)

Each discovered URL is validated using a HEAD request (not GET).

Purpose:
• Confirm URL is active
• Detect 403 bot protection
• Avoid unnecessary full requests

Lightweight and fast validation step.

## Comparison

Compares URLs found via:

• HTTP path
• Browserless path

Highlights URLs found only through browser rendering.

Helps determine if Browserless adds meaningful coverage.

## Final Output

Merges HTTP + Browserless results.

• Removes duplicates
• Outputs validated URLs
• Shows final content preview

## PDF Preview

If a URL points to a PDF file, it is routed to a separate extraction path.

• Extracts raw text from the PDF
• Previews the extracted content

Allows users to confirm that PDF content can be successfully read before running a full workflow.
